gauge.name: Marmaton River at Fort Scott

impacts.statement: this is the record stage level reached on October 3, 1986.
flood waters reach the ground floor of the Burlington Northern Railroad Office on Wall Street.
flood waters begin to spill over the Railroad crossing along Highway 54, or Wall Street, east of Key Industries.
flood waters reach the Fire Station on West Highway 54.
flood waters begin to impact Hills Service Center south of the National Avenue Bridge at the gage site.

flood waters begin to impact the ground floor of the former Red Barn Restaurant, and the Red Ram Motel along West Highway 54. In addition, flood waters begin to impact the Fort Scott RV Park, north of West Highway 54.
flood waters begin to spill over the bridge deck at the Marmaton River, along North National Avenue.
flood waters reach the bottom of the National Avenue Bridge at the gage site.
flood waters begin to affect low areas at the KOA Campground, north of West Highway 54, and Key Industries on Wall Street.
flood waters may reach the ground floor of businesses on West Highway 54 and North National Avenue. In addition, West Highway 54 will close due to flood waters.

flood waters reach the parking lot along Highway 54 West and North National Avenue.  Flood waters will also cut off access to the community of Walnut Hill, which is south of Highway 54, and west of National Avenue. In addition, flood waters rise to a depth of six inches at the junction of Highway 54 and Wall Street, east of downtown Fort Scott.

flood waters cause Wall Street at 3rd and Clark to close, which cuts off fire protection on the east side of Fort Scott, Kansas.  In addition, Buck Run Creek that flows along Highway 69, floods portions of Clark and Wall Street due to backwater of the Marmaton River.
major flood stage. Flood waters impact all Belltown businesses to the ground floor, except Smith & Chambers, but all roads are not accessible to this area.
all roads leading into the Belltown area, north of the Marmaton River are impacted by flood waters, and no access is available.
minor flooding begins in the Belltown area, north of the Marmaton River and west of National Avenue.
flood waters begin to impact low areas on Happy Hollow Road, Maple Road and 2nd Street north and west of downtown Fort Scott.

minor flooding begins along Mill Creek north of town due to backwater effects of the Marmaton River. Flood waters also spill across the field by the Red Barn Restaurant, on the north side of town, at the new Highway 69 Bridge.

flood waters begin to impact the county road, or low water crossing on Mill Creek, between Maple Road and Highway 54, on 205th street, northwest of town. This occurs based on backwater affects on the Marmaton River.

hydronotes.statement: Gauge reading may be affected by ice.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
